title: identity.unmerged.v1
description: SPEC A.2.4 (WA-19, AMD-08/AMD-09) — the admin unmerge (merge-reversal) envelope. Registered in Apicurio as brain/identity.unmerged.v1 (JSON Schema artifact under the FULL_TRANSITIVE rule, per AMD-03) — the ONE convention-following sibling AMD-08 sanctions on the live {env}.identity.*.v1 lane for a mutation class no existing topic fits. Partition key = brand_id (tenant-first, I-S01). PII discipline (I-S02): payload carries brain_ids, merge_id, rule_version, an operator actor id and an optional free-text reason — NEVER a raw email/phone/name/address. AMD-09: survivor = the lowest-UUID canonical the original merge chose (unchanged); the unmerge restores the ABSORBED id (restored_brain_id) to independent existence and closes the merge interval. Downstream: the journey re-version job un-reverts the affected brain_ids; the identity-change recompute consumer marks both dirty.

brand_id.description: Tenant key — brand_id-first on every event (I-S01).

merge_id.description: The ORIGINAL merge this unmerge reverses (identity_merge_event.merge_id / Neo4j MergeEvent.merge_id).

canonical_brain_id.description: The surviving canonical identity the absorbed id had been folded into (unchanged by the split; the merge survivor, AMD-09 lowest-UUID).

restored_brain_id.description: The identity being split back OUT and restored to independent existence (the former absorbed id).

rule_version.description: Resolution rule-set version for the reversal (audit/replay), e.g. 'v1-admin-unmerge'.

actor.description: The operator who triggered the unmerge (the auth principal user id — audit, never raw PII).

reason.description: Optional operator-supplied reason for the reversal (free text, no PII by contract).
